apollo-esk-1.description:
The shared I²C bus for the kit's sensor modules. Configure it once
and the AHT20 module uses it, along with Apollo's optional breakout
boards for adding more I²C sensors.
apollo-esk-1.name: I²C Bus (sensor modules)

apollo-esk-1.description:
Temperature and humidity module shipped with the starter kit. Plug
it into either FPC connector and add the I²C Bus component so it
can talk to the board. Accurate to ±0.3°C and ±2% humidity, useful
for tracking room comfort or humidity in places like a bathroom or
basement.
apollo-esk-1.name: AHT20 Temperature & Humidity Module

apollo-esk-1.description:
Onboard fuel gauge for monitoring the LiPo battery's voltage and
charge level.
apollo-esk-1.name: Battery Fuel Gauge (MAX17048)

apollo-esk-1.description:
Internal switch that powers the FPC accessory connectors at boot.
You don't need to touch this. It's set to always-on so plug-in
modules get power before they start up.
apollo-esk-1.name: Accessory Power Rail

apollo-esk-1.description:
Motion sensor module included with the starter kit. Detects motion
in rooms like a kitchen, bathroom, or hallway, perfect for
triggering lights or other automations.
apollo-esk-1.name: Motion Module (MH-SR602)

apollo-esk-1.description:
The physical button on the dev board. Use it as a secondary button
in automations, or hold it during power-on to enter boot mode for
flashing.
apollo-esk-1.name: Boot Button (onboard)

apollo-esk-1.description:
Pushbutton module shipped with the starter kit. Plug it into the
FPC connector (GPIO 6) and use it as a button input in automations.
apollo-esk-1.name: Button Module

apollo-esk-1.description:
The 10 RGB LEDs by themselves, without the buzzer parts. If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the LEDs along with the buzzer and
ringtone player.
apollo-esk-1.name: RGB LEDs (module)

apollo-esk-1.description: The onboard RGB LED on the ESP32-C6 module.
apollo-esk-1.name: Onboard RGB LED

apollo-esk-1.description:
The piezo buzzer output by itself, without the LEDs. If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the buzzer along with the LEDs and
ringtone player.
apollo-esk-1.name: Piezo Buzzer (module)

apollo-esk-1.description:
Plays ringtones through a piezo buzzer (RTTTL format). If you have
the whole RGB + Buzzer module, add the "RGB LED + Buzzer Module"
bundle instead. It includes the ringtone player along with the
LEDs and buzzer.
apollo-esk-1.name: RTTTL Player (buzzer)

athom-smart-plug-v3.description:
16 A relay hard-wired to GPIO6. The pin is fixed; the rest of the
fields are yours to set.
athom-smart-plug-v3.name: Onboard Relay

athom-smart-plug-v3.description:
Front-panel push button on GPIO9 (active low — uses internal pull-up
via the rich pin form).
athom-smart-plug-v3.name: Front-Panel Button

athom-smart-plug-v3.description:
Low-level GPIO output for the onboard blue LED on GPIO7. Pair with
a ``Status LED Light`` so Home Assistant can toggle it as a light.
athom-smart-plug-v3.name: Status LED Output

athom-smart-plug-v3.description:
Binary light entity for the onboard status LED. Wire its ``output``
to the ``status_led_output`` you added.
athom-smart-plug-v3.name: Status LED Light

athom-smart-plug-v3.description:
Energy-metering chip wired to GPIO3 (CF), GPIO4 (CF1), and GPIO5
(SEL). The pins are physically fixed on this board.
athom-smart-plug-v3.name: HLW8012 Power Monitor

esp32-ethernet-kit.description: Built-in IP101 Ethernet PHY — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-ethernet-kit.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-evb.description: Built-in Ethernet PHY — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-evb.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-gateway.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-p4-pc.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-poe.description: Built-in LAN8720 Ethernet PHY with PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-poe.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-poe-iso.description: Built-in Ethernet PHY with isolated PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-poe-iso.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-poe-iso-wrover.description: Built-in Ethernet PHY with isolated PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-poe-iso-wrover.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-poe-wrover.description: Built-in LAN8720 Ethernet PHY with PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-poe-wrover.name: Onboard Ethernet

esp32-poe2.description: Built-in LAN8720 Ethernet PHY with PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
esp32-poe2.name: Onboard Ethernet

gl_inet_gl_s10_v2.description: Built-in IP101 Ethernet PHY (V2.x) — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
gl_inet_gl_s10_v2.name: Onboard Ethernet

lilygo-t-eth-poe.description: Built-in LAN8720 Ethernet PHY — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
lilygo-t-eth-poe.name: Onboard Ethernet

m5stack-poesp32.description: Built-in IP101 Ethernet PHY with PoE — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
m5stack-poesp32.name: Onboard Ethernet

seeed-xiao-esp32s3-w5500.description: W5500 Ethernet controller (SPI) — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
seeed-xiao-esp32s3-w5500.name: Onboard Ethernet

sonoff-basic.description:
The 10 A AC relay is hard-wired to GPIO12. This recommendation pre-fills
the GPIO and gives it a sensible default name.
sonoff-basic.name: Onboard Relay

sonoff-basic.description:
Front-panel push button on GPIO0 (active low — note ``inverted: true``
plus the internal pull-up).
sonoff-basic.name: Front-Panel Button

sonoff-basic.description:
Low-level GPIO output for the onboard blue LED on GPIO13. Pair with
a ``Status LED Light`` so Home Assistant can toggle it as a light.
sonoff-basic.name: Status LED Output

sonoff-basic.description:
Binary light entity for the onboard status LED. Wire its ``output``
to the ``status_led_output`` you added.
sonoff-basic.name: Status LED Light

waveshare-esp32-s3-eth.description: W5500 Ethernet controller (SPI) — wired networking with no Wi-Fi setup.
waveshare-esp32-s3-eth.name: Onboard Ethernet

waveshare_esp32_c6_geek.description: SPI bus wired to the onboard 1.14in LCD and the microSD slot.
waveshare_esp32_c6_geek.name: Onboard SPI bus

waveshare_esp32_c6_geek.description: 240x135 ST7789 LCD on the onboard SPI bus. Pin mapping follows the community-verified ESPHome config for this board.
waveshare_esp32_c6_geek.name: Onboard 1.14in LCD

waveshare_esp32_p4_module_dev_kit.description: Onboard ESP32-C6 running ESP-Hosted over SDIO gives the radio-less ESP32-P4 Wi-Fi 6. Installed automatically in every new device — the wifi block only works while this is present. If the C6 never comes up on your unit, try active_high false.
waveshare_esp32_p4_module_dev_kit.name: Wi-Fi via ESP32-C6 co-processor

waveshare_esp32_p4_module_dev_kit.description: Built-in IP101 Ethernet PHY (RMII) — selected automatically when a device is created without Wi-Fi credentials.
waveshare_esp32_p4_module_dev_kit.name: Onboard Ethernet

waveshare_esp32_p4_nano.description: Onboard ESP32-C6 running ESP-Hosted over SDIO gives the radio-less ESP32-P4 Wi-Fi 6. Installed automatically in every new device — the wifi block only works while this is present. If the C6 never comes up on your unit, try active_high false.
waveshare_esp32_p4_nano.name: Wi-Fi via ESP32-C6 co-processor

waveshare_esp32_p4_nano.description: Built-in IP101 Ethernet PHY (RMII) — selected automatically when a device is created without Wi-Fi credentials.
waveshare_esp32_p4_nano.name: Onboard Ethernet

waveshare_esp32_p4_pico.description: Onboard ESP32-C6 running ESP-Hosted over SDIO gives the radio-less ESP32-P4 Wi-Fi 6. Installed automatically in every new device — the wifi block only works while this is present. If the C6 never comes up on your unit, try active_high false.

waveshare_esp32_p4_wifi6_dev_kit.name: Wi-Fi via ESP32-C6 co-processor

waveshare_esp32_s3_rgb_matrix.description: The board's HUB75 connector for Waveshare Matrix-RGB panels — pins are fixed by the PCB; set panel_width/panel_height to match the attached panel.
waveshare_esp32_s3_rgb_matrix.name: HUB75 RGB matrix output
